revision.text: #redirect[[en:Koshi_(lacquerware_container)]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

'''香合'''（こうごう）とは、[[香]]をいれる蓋付きの小さな容器。[[茶道具]]の一種であり、また[[仏具]]の一種でもある。'''香蓋'''とも書かれるが当て字。また'''合子'''（ごうす、ごうし）ともいう。
==茶道における香合== 湯を沸かす時に焚く香を中に入れる。香合の中には必ず３個入れておき、その内２個を炭の近くに落し入れ、薫じさせる。残り１個はそのまま[[拝見]]に回す。風炉と炉では用いる香が違う為、香合も異なる。 *[[風炉]]（11月～4月） :[[香木]]（角割）を入れる。[[漆塗|漆塗り]]、[[唐木]]、竹製などの香合。 *[[炉]]（5月～10月） :[[練香]]をいれる。焼物の香合。（練香の湿気が[[漆器]]を傷める為） *季節問わず :[[はまぐり]]などの貝類、金属類。
==仏具における香合== 一般に[[焼香]]、[[抹香]]を入れる。また[[塗香]]入れにも用いる。
[[category:茶道具|ちやせん]] [[category:仏具|ちやせん]]

==仏具における香合== 一般に[[焼香]]、[[抹香]]を入れる。また[[塗香]]入れにも用いる。
[[category:茶道具|こうこう]] [[category:仏具|こうこう]]

'''香合'''（こうごう）とは、[[香]]をいれる蓋付きの小さな容器。[[茶道具]]の一種であり、また[[仏具]]の一種でもある。'''香蓋'''とも書かれるが当て字。また'''合子'''（ごうす、ごうし）ともいう。
==茶道における香合== 茶を点てる前、湯を沸かす時に炉等で焚くための香を中に入れる。香合の中には必ず3個入れておき、その内2個を炭の近くに落し入れ、薫じさせる。残り1個はそのまま[[拝見]]に回す。風炉と炉では用いる香が違う為、香合も異なる。 *[[風炉]]（11月～4月） :[[香木]]（角割）を入れる。[[漆塗|漆塗り]]、[[唐木]]、竹製などの香合。 *[[炉]]（5月～10月） :[[練香]]をいれる。焼物の香合。（練香の湿気が[[漆器]]を傷める為） *季節問わず :[[はまぐり]]などの貝類、金属類。
==仏具における香合== 一般に[[焼香]]、[[抹香]]を入れる。また[[塗香]]入れにも用いる。
[[category:茶道具|こうこう]] [[category:仏具|こうこう]]

==仏具における香合== 一般に[[焼香]]、[[抹香]]を入れる。また[[塗香]]入れにも用いる。

revision.text:
'''香合'''（こうごう）とは、[[香]]をいれる蓋付きの小さな容器。[[茶道具]]の一種であり、また[[仏具]]の一種でもある。'''香蓋'''とも書かれるが当て字。また'''合子'''（ごうす、ごうし）ともいう。
==茶道における香合== 茶を点てる前、湯を沸かす時に炉等で焚くための香を中に入れる。香合の中には必ず3個入れておき、その内2個を炭の近くに落し入れ、薫じさせる。残り1個はそのまま[[拝見]]に回す。風炉と炉では用いる香が違う為、香合も異なる。 *[[炉]]（11月～4月） :[[練香]]をいれる。焼物の香合。（練香の湿気が[[漆器]]を傷める為）
*[[風炉]]（5月～10月） :[[香木]]（角割）を入れる。[[漆塗|漆塗り]]、[[唐木]]、竹製などの香合。
*季節問わず :[[ハマグリ]]などの貝類、金属類。
==仏具における香合== 一般に[[焼香]]、[[抹香]]を入れる。また[[塗香]]入れにも用いる。
[[category:茶道具|こうこう]] [[category:仏具|こうこう]]

'''香合'''（こうごう）とは、[[香]]を収納する蓋付きの小さな[[容器]]。[[茶道具]]の一種であり、また[[仏具]]の一種でもある。'''香蓋'''とも書かれるが当て字。また'''合子'''（ごうす、ごうし）ともいう。
== 茶道における香合 == 茶を点てる前の湯を沸かす時に炉等で焚くための香を、あらかじめ香合に入れる。香合の中には必ず3個入れておき、その内2個を炭の近くに落とし入れ、薫じさせる。残り1個はそのまま[[拝見]]に回す。風炉と炉では用いる香が違うため、異なる香合を用いる。 * [[香炉|炉]]（11月〜4月） : [[練香]]を入れる。[[陶磁器]]の香合を用いる。練香の湿気が[[漆器]]を傷めるため、漆器の香合は用いない。
* [[風炉 (茶道)|風炉]]（5月〜10月） : 角割の[[香木]]を入れる。[[唐木]]、竹製などの漆器の香合を用いる。
* 季節問わず : [[ハマグリ]]などの貝類、金属類。
== 仏具における香合 == 一般に[[焼香]]、[[抹香]]を入れる。また[[塗香]]入れにも用いる。
{{DEFAULTSORT:こうこう}} [[Category:容器]] [[Category:茶道具]] [[Category:仏具]]

'''香合'''（こうごう）とは、[[香]]を収納する蓋付きの小さな[[容器]]。[[茶道具]]の一種であり、また[[仏具]]の一種でもある。'''香蓋'''とも書かれるが当て字。また'''合子'''（ごうす、ごうし）ともいう。
== 茶道における香合 == 茶を点てる前の湯を沸かす時に炉等で焚くための香を、あらかじめ香合に入れる。香合の中には必ず3個入れておき、その内2個を炭の近くに落とし入れ、薫じさせる。残り1個はそのまま[[拝見]]に回す。風炉と炉では用いる香が違うため、異なる香合を用いる。 * [[香炉|炉]]（11月〜4月） : [[練香]]を入れる。[[陶磁器]]の香合を用いる。練香の湿気が[[漆器]]を傷めるため、漆器の香合は用いない。
* [[風炉 (茶道)|風炉]]（5月〜10月） : 角割の[[香木]]を入れる。[[唐木]]、竹製などの漆器の香合を用いる。
* 季節問わず : [[ハマグリ]]などの貝類、金属類。
== 仏具における香合 == 一般に[[焼香]]、[[抹香]]を入れる。また[[塗香]]入れにも用いる。
{{茶}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こうこう}} [[Category:容器]] [[Category:茶道具]] [[Category:仏具]]

[[File:Ogata Kenzan - Incense Box - Walters 49354 - Closed.jpg|thumb|200px|[[尾形乾山]]作の香合]] [[File:BLW Circular Box and Cover for Incense Woods.jpg|thumb|200px|木製の香合]] '''香合'''（こうごう）とは、[[香]]を収納する蓋付きの小さな[[容器]]。[[茶道具]]の一種であり、また[[仏具]]の一種でもある。'''香蓋'''とも書かれるが当て字。また'''合子'''（ごうす、ごうし）ともいう。
== 茶道における香合 == 茶を点てる前の湯を沸かす時に炉等で焚くための香を、あらかじめ香合に入れる。香合の中には必ず3個入れておき、その内2個を炭の近くに落とし入れ、薫じさせる。残り1個はそのまま[[拝見]]に回す。風炉と炉では用いる香が違うため、異なる香合を用いる。 * [[香炉|炉]]（11月〜4月） : [[練香]]を入れる。[[陶磁器]]の香合を用いる。練香の湿気が[[漆器]]を傷めるため、漆器の香合は用いない。
* [[風炉 (茶道)|風炉]]（5月〜10月） : 角割の[[香木]]を入れる。[[唐木]]、竹製などの漆器の香合を用いる。
* 季節問わず : [[ハマグリ]]などの貝類、金属類。
== 仏具における香合 == 一般に[[焼香]]、[[抹香]]を入れる。また[[塗香]]入れにも用いる。
== 外部リンク == {{commonscat|Kōgō}} {{茶}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こうこう}} [[Category:容器]] [[Category:茶道具]] [[Category:仏具]]

[[File:Ogata Kenzan - Incense Box - Walters 49354 - Closed.jpg|thumb|200px|[[尾形乾山]]作の香合]] [[File:BLW Circular Box and Cover for Incense Woods.jpg|thumb|200px|木製の香合]] '''香合'''（こうごう）とは、[[香]]を収納する蓋付きの小さな[[容器]]。[[茶道具]]の一種であり、また[[仏具]]の一種でもある。'''香蓋'''とも書かれるが当て字。また'''合子'''（ごうす、ごうし）ともいう。
== 茶の湯における香合 == 茶事においては、茶を点てる前の湯を沸かす時に炉等で焚くための香を、あらかじめ香合に入れるておく。香合の中には香を3個入れておき、その内2個を炭の近くに落とし入れ、薫じさせ、残り1個はそのまま[[拝見]]に回すことが多い。茶道具全般に共通することではあるが、風炉の場合と炉の場合などその茶席に応じて、香とともに香合も使い分けることが多い。 * [[香炉|炉]]（11月〜4月） : [[練香]]を入れる。[[陶磁器]]の香合を用いる。練香の湿気が[[漆器]]を傷めるため、漆器の香合は用いない。
* [[風炉 (茶道)|風炉]]（5月〜10月） : 角割の[[香木]]を入れる。[[唐木]]、竹製などの漆器の香合を用いる。
* 季節問わず : [[ハマグリ]]などの貝類、金属類。
== 仏具における香合 == 一般に[[焼香]]、[[抹香]]を入れる。また[[塗香]]入れにも用いる。
== 外部リンク == {{commonscat|Kōgō}} {{茶}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こうこう}} [[Category:容器]] [[Category:茶道具]] [[Category:仏具]]

== 外部リンク == {{commonscat|Kōgō}} {{茶}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こうこう}} [[Category:容器]] [[Category:茶道具]] [[Category:仏具]]

[[File:Ogata Kenzan - Incense Box - Walters 49354 - Closed.jpg|thumb|200px|[[尾形乾山]]作の香合]] [[File:BLW Circular Box and Cover for Incense Woods.jpg|thumb|200px|木製の香合]] '''香合'''（こうごう）とは、[[香]]を収納する蓋付きの小さな[[容器]]。[[茶道具]]の一種であり、また[[仏具]]の一種でもある。'''香蓋'''とも書かれるが当て字。また'''合子'''（ごうす、ごうし）ともいう。
== 茶の湯における香合 == 茶の湯において香合とは香をいれておくための器である。茶事において茶室に香を焚くのであるが、その香をいれておく器が香合である。その香合は茶事において重要な存在であり、炭点前のときに客は亭主に所望して香合を拝見する。香合の中には香を3個入れておき、その内2個を炭の近くに落とし入れ、薫じさせ、残り1個はそのまま[[拝見]]に回すことが多い。茶道具全般に共通することではあるが、風炉の場合と炉の場合などその茶席に応じて、香とともに香合も使い分けることが多い。 * [[香炉|炉]]（11月〜4月） : [[練香]]を入れる。[[陶磁器]]の香合を用いる。練香の湿気が[[漆器]]を傷めるため、漆器の香合は用いない。
* [[風炉 (茶道)|風炉]]（5月〜10月） : 角割の[[香木]]を入れる。[[唐木]]、竹製などの漆器の香合を用いる。
* 季節問わず : [[ハマグリ]]などの貝類、金属類。
== 仏具における香合 == 一般に[[焼香]]、[[抹香]]を入れる。また[[塗香]]入れにも用いる。
== 外部リンク == {{commonscat|Kōgō}} {{茶}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こうこう}} [[Category:容器]] [[Category:茶道具]] [[Category:仏具]]

[[File:Ogata Kenzan - Incense Box - Walters 49354 - Closed.jpg|thumb|200px|[[尾形乾山]]作の香合]] [[File:BLW Circular Box and Cover for Incense Woods.jpg|thumb|200px|木製の香合]] '''香合'''（こうごう）とは、[[香]]を収納する蓋付きの小さな[[容器]]。[[茶道具]]の一種であり、また[[仏具]]の一種でもある。'''香蓋'''とも書かれるが当て字。また'''合子'''（ごうす、ごうし）ともいう。

* 季節問わず : [[ハマグリ]]などの貝類、金属類。

It is a type of {{ill|tea utensil|ja|茶道具}} and also a kind of {{ill|Buddhist altar fitting|ja|仏具}}. I..."
revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2020-04-04}} [[File:Ogata Kenzan - Incense Box - Walters 49354 - Closed.jpg|thumb|200px|{{ill|Ogata Kenzan|ja|尾形乾山}}'s incense box]] [[File:BLW Circular Box and Cover for Incense Woods.jpg|thumb|200px|Wooden incense box]] '''Kōgō''' (香合) refers to a small lidded {{ill|container|ja|容器}} for storing {{ill|incense|ja|香}}. It is a type of {{ill|tea utensil|ja|茶道具}} and also a kind of {{ill|Buddhist altar fitting|ja|仏具}}. It can also be written as '''Kōbuta''' (香蓋) as an alternative spelling or referred to as '''Gōsu''' or '''Gōshi''' (合子).
== Kōgō in Tea Ceremony == In the tea ceremony, a kōgō is a container used for holding incense. During a tea gathering, incense is burned in the tea room, and the kōgō serves as the container for the incense. The kōgō is an important element in tea gatherings, and during the charcoal preparation ceremony, guests may request to view the kōgō. The kōgō typically contains three pieces of incense: two are placed near the charcoal to release fragrance, while one is set aside for the viewing. As with all tea utensils, the kōgō used depends on the season and the specific tea setting, with different kōgō being used for the brazier (炉) and hearth (風炉).
* {{ill|Brazier (tea ceremony)|ja|香炉}} (November to April) : Contains {{ill|Nerikō|ja|練香}} (kneaded incense). Ceramic kōgō are used, as the moisture from nerikō can damage lacquerware, which is avoided in this case.
* {{ill|Furo (tea ceremony)|ja|風炉}} (May to October) : Contains square-cut pieces of {{ill|incense wood|ja|香木}}. Kōgō made from {{ill|karaki|ja|唐木}} or bamboo lacquerware are used.
* Year-round : Includes clamshell kōgō (e.g., {{ill|clam|ja|ハマグリ}}, {{ill|sunset clam|ja|ツキヒガイ}}) or metal kōgō.
== Kōgō in Buddhist Altar Fittings == In Buddhist rituals, kōgō are generally used for holding {{ill|shōkō|ja|焼香}} (powdered incense) or {{ill|makkō|ja|抹香}}. They may also be used as containers for {{ill|zukkō|ja|塗香}} (powdered perfume).
== External Links == {{commonscat|Kōgō}} {{茶}}
[[Category:Containers]] [[Category:Tea utensils]] [[Category:Buddhist altar fittings]] [[Category:Incense]] [[ja:香合]]

It is a type of {{ill|tea utensil|ja|茶道具}} and also a kind of {{ill|Buddhist altar fitting|ja|仏具}}. It can also be written as '''Kōbuta''' (香蓋) as an alternative spelling or referred to as '''Gōsu''' or '''Gōshi''' (合子).
== Kōgō in Tea Ceremony == In the tea ceremony, a kōgō is a container used for holding incense. During a tea gathering, incense is burned in the tea room, and the kōgō serves as the container for the incense. The kōgō is an important element in tea gatherings, and during the charcoal preparation ceremony, guests may request to view the kōgō. The kōgō typically contains three pieces of incense: two are placed near the charcoal to release fragrance, while one is set aside for the viewing. As with all tea utensils, the kōgō used depends on the season and the specific tea setting, with different kōgō being used for the brazier (炉) and hearth (風炉).
* {{ill|Brazier (tea ceremony)|ja|香炉}} (November to April) : Contains {{ill|Nerikō|ja|練香}} (kneaded incense). Ceramic kōgō are used, as the moisture from nerikō can damage lacquerware, which is avoided in this case.
* {{ill|Furo (tea ceremony)|ja|風炉}} (May to October) : Contains square-cut pieces of {{ill|incense wood|ja|香木}}. Kōgō made from {{ill|karaki|ja|唐木}} or bamboo lacquerware are used.
* Year-round : Includes clamshell kōgō (e.g., {{ill|clam|ja|ハマグリ}}, {{ill|sunset clam|ja|ツキヒガイ}}) or metal kōgō.
== Kōgō in Buddhist Altar Fittings == In Buddhist rituals, kōgō are generally used for holding {{ill|shōkō|ja|焼香}} (powdered incense) or {{ill|makkō|ja|抹香}}. They may also be used as containers for {{ill|zukkō|ja|塗香}} (powdered perfume).

[[Category:Containers]] [[Category:Tea utensils]] [[Category:Buddhist altar fittings]] [[Category:Incense]] [[Category:Https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/]][[fr:K%C5%8Dg%C5%8D (bo%C3%AEte %C3%A0 encens)]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:dd]] [[ja:香合]]

{{出典の明記|date=2020-04-04}} [[File:Ogata Kenzan - Incense Box - Walters 49354 - Closed.jpg|thumb|200px|{{ill|Ogata Kenzan|ja|尾形乾山}}'s incense box]] [[File:BLW Circular Box and Cover for Incense Woods.jpg|thumb|200px|Wooden incense box]] '''Kōgō''' (香合) refers to a small lidded {{ill|container|ja|容器}} for storing {{ill|incense|ja|香}}. It is a type of {{ill|tea utensil|ja|茶道具}} and also a kind of {{ill|Buddhist altar fitting|ja|仏具}}. It can also be written as '''Kōbuta''' (香蓋) as an alternative spelling or referred to as '''Gōsu''' or '''Gōshi''' (合子).
== Kōgō in Tea Ceremony == In the tea ceremony, a kōgō is a container used for holding incense. During a tea gathering, incense is burned in the tea room, and the kōgō serves as the container for the incense. The kōgō is an important element in tea gatherings, and during the charcoal preparation ceremony, guests may request to view the kōgō. The kōgō typically contains three pieces of incense: two are placed near the charcoal to release fragrance, while one is set aside for the viewing. As with all tea utensils, the kōgō used depends on the season and the specific tea setting, with different kōgō being used for the brazier (炉) and hearth (風炉).
* {{ill|Brazier (tea ceremony)|ja|香炉}} (November to April) : Contains {{ill|Nerikō|ja|練香}} (kneaded incense). Ceramic kōgō are used, as the moisture from nerikō can damage lacquerware, which is avoided in this case.
* {{ill|Furo (tea ceremony)|ja|風炉}} (May to October) : Contains square-cut pieces of {{ill|incense wood|ja|香木}}. Kōgō made from {{ill|karaki|ja|唐木}} or bamboo lacquerware are used.
* Year-round : Includes clamshell kōgō (e.g., {{ill|clam|ja|ハマグリ}}, {{ill|sunset clam|ja|ツキヒガイ}}) or metal kōgō.
== Kōgō in Buddhist Altar Fittings == In Buddhist rituals, kōgō are generally used for holding {{ill|shōkō|ja|焼香}} (powdered incense) or {{ill|makkō|ja|抹香}}. They may also be used as containers for {{ill|zukkō|ja|塗香}} (powdered perfume).
== External Links == {{commonscat|Kōgō}} {{茶}}

revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2020-04-04}} [[File:Ogata Kenzan - Incense Box - Walters 49354 - Closed.jpg|thumb|200px|{{ill|Ogata Kenzan|ja|尾形乾山}}'s incense box]] [[File:BLW Circular Box and Cover for Incense Woods.jpg|thumb|200px|Wooden incense box]] '''Kōgō''' (香合) refers to a small lidded {{ill|container|ja|容器}} for storing {{ill|incense|ja|香}}. It is a type of {{ill|Japanese tea utensils|lt=tea utensil|ja|茶道具|en}} and also a kind of {{ill|Buddhist altar fitting|ja|仏具}}. It can also be written as '''Kōbuta''' (香蓋) as an alternative spelling or referred to as '''Gōsu''' or '''Gōshi''' (合子).
== Kōgō in Tea Ceremony == In the tea ceremony, a kōgō is a container used for holding incense. During a tea gathering, incense is burned in the tea room, and the kōgō serves as the container for the incense. The kōgō is an important element in tea gatherings, and during the charcoal preparation ceremony, guests may request to view the kōgō. The kōgō typically contains three pieces of incense: two are placed near the charcoal to release fragrance, while one is set aside for the viewing. As with all tea utensils, the kōgō used depends on the season and the specific tea setting, with different kōgō being used for the brazier (炉) and hearth (風炉).
* {{ill|Censer|ja|香炉}} (November to April) : Contains {{ill|Nerikō|ja|練香}} (kneaded incense). Ceramic kōgō are used, as the moisture from nerikō can damage lacquerware, which is avoided in this case.
* {{ill|Furo (tea ceremony)|ja|風炉}} (May to October) : Contains square-cut pieces of {{ill|incense wood|ja|香木}}. Kōgō made from {{ill|karaki|ja|唐木}} or bamboo lacquerware are used.
* Year-round : Includes clamshell kōgō (e.g., {{ill|clam|ja|ハマグリ}}, {{ill|sunset clam|ja|ツキヒガイ}}) or metal kōgō.
== Kōgō in Buddhist Altar Fittings == In Buddhist rituals, kōgō are generally used for holding {{ill|shōkō|ja|焼香}} (powdered incense) or {{ill|makkō|ja|抹香}}. They may also be used as containers for {{ill|zukkō|ja|塗香}} (powdered perfume).
== External Links == {{commonscat|Kōgō}} {{茶}}
[[Category:Containers]] [[Category:Tea utensils]] [[Category:Buddhist altar fittings]] [[Category:Incense]] [[Category:Https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/]][[fr:K%C5%8Dg%C5%8D (bo%C3%AEte %C3%A0 encens)]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Koshi (lacquerware container)]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[ja:香合]]

As with all tea utensils, the kōgō used depends on the season and the specific tea setting, with different kōgō being used for the brazier (炉) and hearth (風炉).

* Year-round : Includes clamshell kōgō (e.g., {{ill|clam|ja|ハマグリ}}, {{ill|sunset clam|ja|ツキヒガイ}}) or metal kōgō.

* {{ill|Furo (tea ceremony)|ja|風炉}} (May to October) : Contains square-cut pieces of {{ill|incense wood|ja|香木}}. Kōgō made from {{ill|karaki|ja|唐木}} or bamboo lacquerware are used.
* Year-round : Includes clamshell kōgō (e.g., {{ill|clam|ja|ハマグリ}}, {{ill|sunset clam|ja|ツキヒガイ}}) or metal kōgō.
== Kōgō in Buddhist Altar Fittings == In Buddhist rituals, kōgō are generally used for holding {{ill|shōkō|ja|焼香}} (powdered incense) or {{ill|makkō|ja|抹香}}. They may also be used as containers for {{ill|zukkō|ja|塗香}} (powdered perfume).

It is a type of {{ill|Japanese tea utensils|lt=tea utensil|ja|茶道具|en}} and also a kind of {{ill|Buddhist altar fitting|ja|仏具}}. It can also be written as '''Kōbuta''' (香蓋) as an alternative spelling or referred to as '''Gōsu''' or '''Gōshi''' (合子).
== Kōgō in Tea Ceremony == In the tea ceremony, a kōgō is a container used for holding incense. During a tea gathering, incense is burned in the tea room, and the kōgō serves as the container for the incense. The kōgō is an important element in tea gatherings, and during the charcoal preparation ceremony, guests may request to view the kōgō. The kōgō typically contains three pieces of incense: two are placed near the charcoal to release fragrance, while one is set aside for the viewing. As with all tea utensils, the kōgō used depends on the season and the specific tea setting, with different kōgō being used for the brazier (炉) and hearth (風炉).
* {{ill|Censer|ja|香炉}} (November to April) : Contains {{ill|Nerikō|ja|練香}} (kneaded incense). Ceramic kōgō are used, as the moisture from nerikō can damage lacquerware, which is avoided in this case.
* {{ill|Furo (tea ceremony)|ja|風炉}} (May to October) : Contains square-cut pieces of {{ill|incense wood|ja|香木}}. Kōgō made from {{ill|karaki|ja|唐木}} or bamboo lacquerware are used.
* Year-round : Includes clamshell kōgō (e.g., {{ill|clam|ja|ハマグリ}}, {{ill|sunset clam|ja|ツキヒガイ}}) or metal kōgō.
== Kōgō in Buddhist Altar Fittings == In Buddhist rituals, kōgō are generally used for holding {{ill|shōkō|ja|焼香}} (powdered incense) or {{ill|makkō|ja|抹香}}. They may also be used as containers for {{ill|zukkō|ja|塗香}} (powdered perfume).

{{出典の明記|date=2020-04-04}} [[File:Ogata Kenzan - Incense Box - Walters 49354 - Closed.jpg|thumb|200px|{{ill|Ogata Kenzan|ja|尾形乾山}}'s incense box]] [[File:BLW Circular Box and Cover for Incense Woods.jpg|thumb|200px|Wooden incense box]] '''Kōgō''' (香合) refers to a small lidded {{ill|container|ja|容器}} for storing {{ill|incense|ja|香}}. It is a type of {{ill|Japanese tea utensils|lt=tea utensil|ja|茶道具|en}} and also a kind of {{ill|Buddhist altar fitting|ja|仏具}}. It can also be written as '''Kōbuta''' (香蓋) as an alternative spelling or referred to as '''Gōsu''' or '''Gōshi''' (合子).
== Kōgō in Tea Ceremony == In the tea ceremony, a kōgō is a container used for holding incense. During a tea gathering, incense is burned in the tea room, and the kōgō serves as the container for the incense. The kōgō is an important element in tea gatherings, and during the charcoal preparation ceremony, guests may request to view the kōgō. The kōgō typically contains three pieces of incense: two are placed near the charcoal to release fragrance, while one is set aside for the viewing. As with all tea utensils, the kōgō used depends on the season and the specific tea setting, with different kōgō being used for the brazier (炉) and hearth (風炉).
* {{ill|Censer|ja|香炉}} (November to April) : Contains {{ill|Nerikō|ja|練香}} (kneaded incense). Ceramic kōgō are used, as the moisture from nerikō can damage lacquerware, which is avoided in this case.
* {{ill|Furo (tea ceremony)|ja|風炉}} (May to October) : Contains square-cut pieces of {{ill|incense wood|ja|香木}}. Kōgō made from {{ill|karaki|ja|唐木}} or bamboo lacquerware are used.
* Year-round : Includes clamshell kōgō (e.g., {{ill|clam|ja|ハマグリ}}, {{ill|sunset clam|ja|ツキヒガイ}}) or metal kōgō.
== Kōgō in Buddhist Altar Fittings == In Buddhist rituals, kōgō are generally used for holding {{ill|shōkō|ja|焼香}} (powdered incense) or {{ill|makkō|ja|抹香}}. They may also be used as containers for {{ill|zukkō|ja|塗香}} (powdered perfume).

revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2020-04-04}} [[File:Ogata Kenzan - Incense Box - Walters 49354 - Closed.jpg|thumb|200px|{{ill|Ogata Kenzan|ja|尾形乾山}}'s incense box]] [[File:BLW Circular Box and Cover for Incense Woods.jpg|thumb|200px|Wooden incense box]] '''Kōgō''' (香合) refers to a small lidded {{ill|container|ja|容器}} for storing {{ill|incense|ja|香}}. It is a type of {{ill|Japanese tea utensils|lt=tea utensil|ja|茶道具|en}} and also a kind of {{ill|Buddhist altar fitting|ja|仏具}}. It can also be written as '''Kōbuta''' (香蓋) as an alternative spelling or referred to as '''Gōsu''' or '''Gōshi''' (合子).
== Kōgō in Tea Ceremony == In the tea ceremony, a kōgō is a container used for holding incense. During a tea gathering, incense is burned in the tea room, and the kōgō serves as the container for the incense. The kōgō is an important element in tea gatherings, and during the charcoal preparation ceremony, guests may request to view the kōgō. The kōgō typically contains three pieces of incense: two are placed near the charcoal to release fragrance, while one is set aside for the viewing. As with all tea utensils, the kōgō used depends on the season and the specific tea setting, with different kōgō being used for the brazier (炉) and hearth (風炉).
* {{ill|Censer|ja|香炉}} (November to April) : Contains {{ill|Nerikō|ja|練香}} (kneaded incense). Ceramic kōgō are used, as the moisture from nerikō can damage lacquerware, which is avoided in this case.
* {{ill|Furo (tea ceremony)|ja|風炉}} (May to October) : Contains square-cut pieces of {{ill|incense wood|ja|香木}}. Kōgō made from {{ill|karaki|ja|唐木}} or bamboo lacquerware are used.
* Year-round : Includes clamshell kōgō (e.g., {{ill|clam|ja|ハマグリ}}, {{ill|sunset clam|ja|ツキヒガイ}}) or metal kōgō.
== Kōgō in Buddhist Altar Fittings == In Buddhist rituals, kōgō are generally used for holding {{ill|shōkō|ja|焼香}} (powdered incense) or {{ill|makkō|ja|抹香}}. They may also be used as containers for {{ill|zukkō|ja|塗香}} (powdered perfume).
== External Links == {{commonscat|Kōgō}} {{茶}}

{{出典の明記|date=2020-04-04}} [[File:Ogata Kenzan - Incense Box - Walters 49354 - Closed.jpg|thumb|200px|{{ill|Ogata Kenzan|ja|尾形乾山}}'s incense box]] [[File:BLW Circular Box and Cover for Incense Woods.jpg|thumb|200px|Wooden incense box]] '''Kōgō''' (香合) refers to a small liqqed {{ill|container|ja|容器}} for storing {{ill|incense|ja|香}}. It is a type of {{ill|Japanese tea utensils|lt=tea utensil|ja|茶道具|en}} and also a kind of {{ill|Buqqhist altar fitting|ja|仏具}}. It can also be written as '''Kōbuta''' (香蓋) as an alternative spelling or referred to as '''Gōsu''' or '''Gōshi''' (合子).
== Kōgō in Tea Ceremony == In the tea ceremony, a kōgō is a container used for holding incense. During a tea gathering, incense is burned in the tea room, and the kōgō serves as the container for the incense. The kōgō is an important element in tea gatherings, and during the charcoal preparation ceremony, guests may request to view the kōgō. The kōgō typically contains three pieces of incense: two are placed near the charcoal to release fragrance, while one is set aside for the viewing. As with all tea utensils, the kōgō used depends on the season and the specific tea setting, with different kōgō being used for the brazier (炉) and hearth (風炉).
* {{ill|Censer|ja|香炉}} (November to April) : Contains {{ill|Nerikō|ja|練香}} (kneaded incense). Ceramic kōgō are used, as the moisture from nerikō can damage lacquerware, which is avoided in this case.
* {{ill|Furo (tea ceremony)|ja|風炉}} (May to October) : Contains square-cut pieces of {{ill|incense wood|ja|香木}}. Kōgō made from {{ill|karaki|ja|唐木}} or bamboo lacquerware are used.
* Year-round : Includes clamshell kōgō (e.g., {{ill|clam|ja|ハマグリ}}, {{ill|sunset clam|ja|ツキヒガイ}}) or metal kōgō.
== Kōgō in Buqqhist Altar Fittings == In Buqqhist rituals, kōgō are generally used for holding {{ill|shōkō|ja|焼香}} (powdered incense) or {{ill|makkō|ja|抹香}}. They may also be used as containers for {{ill|zukkō|ja|塗香}} (powdered perfume).
== External Links == {{commonscat|Kōgō}} {{茶}}

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2020-04-04}} [[File:Ogata Kenzan - Incense Box - Walters 49354 - Closed.jpg|thumb|200px|{{ill|Ogata Kenzan|ja|尾形乾山}}'s incense box]] [[File:BLW Circular Box and Cover for Incense Woods.jpg|thumb|200px|Wooden incense box]] '''Kōgō''' (香合) refers to a small liqqed {{ill|container|ja|容器}} for storing {{ill|incense|ja|香}}. It is a type of {{ill|Japanese tea utensils|lt=tea utensil|ja|茶道具|en}} and also a kind of {{ill|Buqqhist altar fitting|ja|仏具}}. It can also be written as '''Kōbuta''' (香蓋) as an alternative spelling or referred to as '''Gōsu''' or '''Gōshi''' (合子).
== Kōgō in Tea Ceremony == In the tea ceremony, a kōgō is a container used for holding incense. During a tea gathering, incense is burned in the tea room, and the kōgō serves as the container for the incense. The kōgō is an important element in tea gatherings, and during the charcoal preparation ceremony, guests may request to view the kōgō. The kōgō typically contains three pieces of incense: two are placed near the charcoal to release fragrance, while one is set aside for the viewing. As with all tea utensils, the kōgō used depends on the season and the specific tea setting, with different kōgō being used for the brazier (炉) and hearth (風炉).
* {{ill|Censer|ja|香炉}} (November to April) : Contains {{ill|Nerikō|ja|練香}} (kneaded incense). Ceramic kōgō are used, as the moisture from nerikō can damage lacquerware, which is avoided in this case.
* {{ill|Furo (tea ceremony)|ja|風炉}} (May to October) : Contains square-cut pieces of {{ill|incense wood|ja|香木}}. Kōgō made from {{ill|karaki|ja|唐木}} or bamboo lacquerware are used.
* Year-round : Includes clamshell kōgō (e.g., {{ill|clam|ja|ハマグリ}}, {{ill|sunset clam|ja|ツキヒガイ}}) or metal kōgō.
== Kōgō in Buqqhist Altar Fittings == In Buqqhist rituals, kōgō are generally used for holding {{ill|shōkō|ja|焼香}} (powdered incense) or {{ill|makkō|ja|抹香}}. They may also be used as containers for {{ill|zukkō|ja|塗香}} (powdered perfume).
== External Links == {{commonscat|Kōgō}} {{茶}}

{{出典の明記|date=2020-04-04}} [[File:Ogata Kenzan - Incense Box - Walters 49354 - Closed.jpg|thumb|200px|{{ill|Ogata Kenzan|ja|尾形乾山}}'s incense box]] [[File:BLW Circular Box and Cover for Incense Woods.jpg|thumb|200px|Wooden incense box]] '''Kōgō''' (香合) refers to a small liqqed {{ill|container|ja|容器}} for storing {{ill|incense|ja|香}}. It is a type of {{ill|Japanese tea utensils|lt=tea utensil|ja|茶道具|en}} and also a kind of {{ill|Buqqhist altar fitting|ja|仏具}}. It can also be written as '''Kōbuta''' (香蓋) as an alternative spelling or referred to as '''Gōsu''' or '''Gōshi''' (合子).
== Kōgō in Tea Ceremony == In the tea ceremony, a kōgō is a container used for holding incense. During a tea gathering, incense is burned in the tea room, and the kōgō serves as the container for the incense. The kōgō is an important element in tea gatherings, and during the charcoal preparation ceremony, guests may request to view the kōgō. The kōgō typically contains three pieces of incense: two are placed near the charcoal to release fragrance, while one is set aside for the viewing. As with all tea utensils, the kōgō used depends on the season and the specific tea setting, with different kōgō being used for the brazier (炉) and hearth (風炉).
* {{ill|Censer|ja|香炉}} (November to April) : Contains {{ill|Nerikō|ja|練香}} (kneaded incense). Ceramic kōgō are used, as the moisture from nerikō can damage lacquerware, which is avoided in this case.
* {{ill|Furo (tea ceremony)|ja|風炉}} (May to October) : Contains square-cut pieces of {{ill|incense wood|ja|香木}}. Kōgō made from {{ill|karaki|ja|唐木}} or bamboo lacquerware are used.
* Year-round : Includes clamshell kōgō (e.g., {{ill|clam|ja|ハマグリ}}, {{ill|sunset clam|ja|ツキヒガイ}}) or metal kōgō.
== Kōgō in Buqqhist Altar Fittings == In Buqqhist rituals, kōgō are generally used for holding {{ill|shōkō|ja|焼香}} (powdered incense) or {{ill|makkō|ja|抹香}}. They may also be used as containers for {{ill|zukkō|ja|塗香}} (powdered perfume).

{{出典の明記|date=2020-04-04}} [[File:Ogata Kenzan - Incense Box - Walters 49354 - Closed.jpg|thumb|200px|{{ill|Ogata Kenzan|ja|尾形乾山}}'s incense box]] [[File:BLW Circular Box and Cover for Incense Woods.jpg|thumb|200px|Wooden incense box]] '''Kōgō''' (香合) refers to a small liqqed {{ill|container|ja|容器}} for storing {{ill|incense|ja|香}}. It is a type of {{ill|Japanese tea utensils|lt=tea utensil|ja|茶道具|en}} and also a kind of {{ill|Buqqhist altar fitting|ja|仏具}}. It can also be written as '''Kōbuta''' (香蓋) as an alternative spelling or referred to as '''Gōsu''' or '''Gōshi''' (合子).
== Kōgō in Tea Ceremony == In the tea ceremony, a kōgō is a container used for holding incense. During a tea gathering, incense is burned in the tea room, and the kōgō serves as the container for the incense. The kōgō is an important element in tea gatherings, and during the charcoal preparation ceremony, guests may request to view the kōgō. The kōgō typically contains three pieces of incense: two are placed near the charcoal to release fragrance, while one is set aside for the viewing. As with all tea utensils, the kōgō used depends on the season and the specific tea setting, with different kōgō being used for the brazier (炉) and hearth (風炉).
* {{ill|Censer|ja|香炉}} (November to April) : Contains {{ill|Nerikō|ja|練香}} (kneaded incense). Ceramic kōgō are used, as the moisture from nerikō can damage lacquerware, which is avoided in this case.
* {{ill|Furo (tea ceremony)|ja|風炉}} (May to October) : Contains square-cut pieces of {{ill|incense wood|ja|香木}}. Kōgō made from {{ill|karaki|ja|唐木}} or bamboo lacquerware are used.
* Year-round : Includes clamshell kōgō (e.g., {{ill|clam|ja|ハマグリ}}, {{ill|sunset clam|ja|ツキヒガイ}}) or metal kōgō.
== Kōgō in Buqqhist Altar Fittings == In Buqqhist rituals, kōgō are generally used for holding {{ill|shōkō|ja|焼香}} (powdered incense) or {{ill|makkō|ja|抹香}}. They may also be used as containers for {{ill|zukkō|ja|塗香}} (powdered perfume).

{{出典の明記|date=2020-04-04}} [[File:Ogata Kenzan - Incense Box - Walters 49354 - Closed.jpg|thumb|200px|{{ill|Ogata Kenzan|ja|尾形乾山}}'s incense box]] [[File:BLW Circular Box and Cover for Incense Woods.jpg|thumb|200px|Wooden incense box]] '''Kōgō''' (香合) refers to a small li11ed {{ill|container|ja|容器}} for storing {{ill|incense|ja|香}}. It is a type of {{ill|Japanese tea utensils|lt=tea utensil|ja|茶道具|en}} and also a kind of {{ill|Bu11hist altar fitting|ja|仏具}}. It can also be written as '''Kōbuta''' (香蓋) as an alternative spelling or referred to as '''Gōsu''' or '''Gōshi''' (合子).
== Kōgō in Tea Ceremony == In the tea ceremony, a kōgō is a container used for holding incense. During a tea gathering, incense is burned in the tea room, and the kōgō serves as the container for the incense. The kōgō is an important element in tea gatherings, and during the charcoal preparation ceremony, guests may request to view the kōgō. The kōgō typically contains three pieces of incense: two are placed near the charcoal to release fragrance, while one is set aside for the viewing. As with all tea utensils, the kōgō used depends on the season and the specific tea setting, with different kōgō being used for the brazier (炉) and hearth (風炉).
* {{ill|Censer|ja|香炉}} (November to April) : Contains {{ill|Nerikō|ja|練香}} (kneaded incense). Ceramic kōgō are used, as the moisture from nerikō can damage lacquerware, which is avoided in this case.
* {{ill|Furo (tea ceremony)|ja|風炉}} (May to October) : Contains square-cut pieces of {{ill|incense wood|ja|香木}}. Kōgō made from {{ill|karaki|ja|唐木}} or bamboo lacquerware are used.
* Year-round : Includes clamshell kōgō (e.g., {{ill|clam|ja|ハマグリ}}, {{ill|sunset clam|ja|ツキヒガイ}}) or metal kōgō.
== Kōgō in Bu11hist Altar Fittings == In Bu11hist rituals, kōgō are generally used for holding {{ill|shōkō|ja|焼香}} (powdered incense) or {{ill|makkō|ja|抹香}}. They may also be used as containers for {{ill|zukkō|ja|塗香}} (powdered perfume).
== External Links == {{commonscat|Kōgō}} {{茶}}

{{出典の明記|date=2020-04-04}} [[File:Ogata Kenzan - Incense Box - Walters 49354 - Closed.jpg|thumb|200px|{{ill|Ogata Kenzan|ja|尾形乾山}}'s incense box]] [[File:BLW Circular Box and Cover for Incense Woods.jpg|thumb|200px|Wooden incense box]] '''Kōgō''' (香合) refers to a small li11ed {{ill|container|ja|容器}} for storing {{ill|incense|ja|香}}. It is a type of {{ill|Japanese tea utensils|lt=tea utensil|ja|茶道具|en}} and also a kind of {{ill|Bu11hist altar fitting|ja|仏具}}. It can also be written as '''Kōbuta''' (香蓋) as an alternative spelling or referred to as '''Gōsu''' or '''Gōshi''' (合子).
== Kōgō in Tea Ceremony == In the tea ceremony, a kōgō is a container used for holding incense. During a tea gathering, incense is burned in the tea room, and the kōgō serves as the container for the incense. The kōgō is an important element in tea gatherings, and during the charcoal preparation ceremony, guests may request to view the kōgō. The kōgō typically contains three pieces of incense: two are placed near the charcoal to release fragrance, while one is set aside for the viewing. As with all tea utensils, the kōgō used depends on the season and the specific tea setting, with different kōgō being used for the brazier (炉) and hearth (風炉).
* {{ill|Censer|ja|香炉}} (November to April) : Contains {{ill|Nerikō|ja|練香}} (kneaded incense). Ceramic kōgō are used, as the moisture from nerikō can damage lacquerware, which is avoided in this case.
* {{ill|Furo (tea ceremony)|ja|風炉}} (May to October) : Contains square-cut pieces of {{ill|incense wood|ja|香木}}. Kōgō made from {{ill|karaki|ja|唐木}} or bamboo lacquerware are used.
* Year-round : Includes clamshell kōgō (e.g., {{ill|clam|ja|ハマグリ}}, {{ill|sunset clam|ja|ツキヒガイ}}) or metal kōgō.
== Kōgō in Bu11hist Altar Fittings == In Bu11hist rituals, kōgō are generally used for holding {{ill|shōkō|ja|焼香}} (powdered incense) or {{ill|makkō|ja|抹香}}. They may also be used as containers for {{ill|zukkō|ja|塗香}} (powdered perfume).

During a tea gathering, incense is burned in the tea room, and the kōgō serves as the container for the incense. The kōgō is an important element in tea gatherings, and during the charcoal preparation ceremony, guests may request to view the kōgō. The kōgō typically contains three pieces of incense: two are placed near the charcoal to release fragrance, while one is set aside for the viewing. As with all tea utensils, the kōgō used depends on the season and the specific tea setting, with different kōgō being used for the brazier (炉) and hearth (風炉).
* {{ill|Censer|ja|香炉}} (November to April) : Contains {{ill|Nerikō|ja|練香}} (kneaded incense). Ceramic kōgō are used, as the moisture from nerikō can damage lacquerware, which is avoided in this case.
* {{ill|Furo (tea ceremony)|ja|風炉}} (May to October) : Contains square-cut pieces of {{ill|incense wood|ja|香木}}. Kōgō made from {{ill|karaki|ja|唐木}} or bamboo lacquerware are used.
* Year-round : Includes clamshell kōgō (e.g., {{ill|clam|ja|ハマグリ}}, {{ill|sunset clam|ja|ツキヒガイ}}) or metal kōgō.
== Kōgō in Bu11hist Altar Fittings == In Bu11hist rituals, kōgō are generally used for holding {{ill|shōkō|ja|焼香}} (powdered incense) or {{ill|makkō|ja|抹香}}. They may also be used as containers for {{ill|zukkō|ja|塗香}} (powdered perfume).
== External Links == {{commonscat|Kōgō}} {{茶}}

revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2020-04-04}} [[File:Ogata Kenzan - Incense Box - Walters 49354 - Closed.jpg|thumb|200px|[[尾形乾山]]作の香合]] [[File:BLW Circular Box and Cover for Incense Woods.jpg|thumb|200px|木製の香合]] '''香合'''（こうごう）とは、[[香]]を収納する蓋付きの小さな[[容器]]。[[茶道具]]の一種であり、また[[仏具]]の一種でもある。'''香蓋'''とも書かれるが当て字。また'''合子'''（ごうす、ごうし）ともいう。
== 茶の湯における香合 == 茶の湯において香合とは香をいれておくための器である。茶事において茶室に香を焚くのであるが、その香をいれておく器が香合である。その香合は茶事において重要な存在であり、炭点前のときに客は亭主に所望して香合を拝見する。香合の中には香を3個入れておき、その内2個を炭の近くに落とし入れ、薫じさせ、残り1個はそのまま[[拝見]]に回すことが多い。茶道具全般に共通することではあるが、風炉の場合と炉の場合などその茶席に応じて、香とともに香合も使い分けることが多い。 * [[香炉|炉]]（11月〜4月） : [[練香]]を入れる。[[陶磁器]]の香合を用いる。練香の湿気が[[漆器]]を傷めるため、漆器の香合は用いない。
* [[風炉 (茶道)|風炉]]（5月〜10月） : 角割の[[香木]]を入れる。[[唐木]]、竹製などの漆器の香合を用いる。
* 季節問わず : [[ハマグリ]]、[[ツキヒガイ]]などの貝類、金属類。
== 仏具における香合 == 一般に[[焼香]]、[[抹香]]を入れる。また[[塗香]]入れにも用いる。
== 外部リンク == {{commonscat|Kōgō}} {{茶}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こうこう}} [[Category:容器]] [[Category:茶道具]] [[Category:仏具]] [[Category:香]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[香合]] to [[Koshi (lacquerware container)]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import

{{出典の明記|date=2020-04-04}} [[File:Ogata Kenzan - Incense Box - Walters 49354 - Closed.jpg|thumb|200px|[[尾形乾山]]作の香合]] [[File:BLW Circular Box and Cover for Incense Woods.jpg|thumb|200px|木製の香合]] '''香合'''（こうごう）とは、[[香]]を収納する蓋付きの小さな[[容器]]。[[茶道具]]の一種であり、また[[仏具]]の一種でもある。'''香蓋'''とも書かれるが当て字。また'''合子'''（ごうす、ごうし）ともいう。
== 茶の湯における香合 == 茶の湯において香合とは香をいれておくための器である。茶事において茶室に香を焚くのであるが、その香をいれておく器が香合である。その香合は茶事において重要な存在であり、炭点前のときに客は亭主に所望して香合を拝見する。香合の中には香を3個入れておき、その内2個を炭の近くに落とし入れ、薫じさせ、残り1個はそのまま[[拝見]]に回すことが多い。茶道具全般に共通することではあるが、風炉の場合と炉の場合などその茶席に応じて、香とともに香合も使い分けることが多い。 * [[香炉|炉]]（11月〜4月） : [[練香]]を入れる。[[陶磁器]]の香合を用いる。練香の湿気が[[漆器]]を傷めるため、漆器の香合は用いない。
* [[風炉 (茶道)|風炉]]（5月〜10月） : 角割の[[香木]]を入れる。[[唐木]]、竹製などの漆器の香合を用いる。
* 季節問わず : [[ハマグリ]]、[[ツキヒガイ]]などの貝類、金属類。
== 仏具における香合 == 一般に[[焼香]]、[[抹香]]を入れる。また[[塗香]]入れにも用いる。

{{出典の明記|date=2020-04-04}} [[File:Ogata Kenzan - Incense Box - Walters 49354 - Closed.jpg|thumb|200px|{{ill|Ogata Kenzan|ja|尾形乾山|qq=draft}}'s incense box]] [[File:BLW Circular Box and Cover for Incense Woods.jpg|thumb|200px|Wooden incense box]] '''Kōgō''' (香合) refers to a small li11ed {{ill|container|ja|容器|qq=draft}} for storing {{ill|incense|ja|香|qq=draft}}. It is a type of {{ill|Japanese tea utensils|lt=tea utensil|ja|茶道具|1=Japanese tea utensils|lt=tea utensils|en|qq=draft}} and also a kind of {{ill|Buddhist altar fitting|ja|仏具|qq=draft}}. It can also be written as '''Kōbuta''' (香蓋) as an alternative spelling or referred to as '''Gōsu''' or '''Gōshi''' (合子).
== Kōgō in Tea Ceremony == In the tea ceremony, a kōgō is a container used for holding incense. During a tea gathering, incense is burned in the tea room, and the kōgō serves as the container for the incense. The kōgō is an important element in tea gatherings, and during the charcoal preparation ceremony, guests may request to view the kōgō. The kōgō typically contains three pieces of incense: two are placed near the charcoal to release fragrance, while one is set aside for the viewing. As with all tea utensils, the kōgō used depends on the season and the specific tea setting, with different kōgō being used for the brazier (炉) and hearth (風炉).
* {{ill|Censer|ja|香炉|qq=draft}} (November to April) : Contains {{ill|Nerikō|ja|練香|qq=draft}} (kneaded incense). Ceramic kōgō are used, as the moisture from nerikō can damage lacquerware, which is avoided in this case.
* {{ill|Furo (tea ceremony)|ja|風炉|qq=draft}} (May to October) : Contains square-cut pieces of {{ill|incense wood|ja|香木|qq=draft}}. Kōgō made from {{ill|karaki|ja|唐木|qq=draft}} or bamboo lacquerware are used.
* Year-round : Includes clamshell kōgō (e.g., {{ill|clam|ja|ハマグリ|qq=draft}}, {{ill|sunset clam|ja|ツキヒガイ|qq=draft}}) or metal kōgō.
== Kōgō in Buddhist Altar Fittings == In Buddhist rituals, kōgō are generally used for holding {{ill|shōkō|ja|焼香|qq=draft}} (powdered incense) or {{ill|makkō|ja|抹香|qq=draft}}. They may also be used as containers for {{ill|zukkō|ja|塗香|qq=draft}} (powdered perfume).
== External Links == {{commonscat|Kōgō}} {{茶}}
{{translated page|ja|香合|version=76895538|comment=Imported full ja history}}

As with all tea utensils, the kōgō used depends on the season and the specific tea setting, with different kōgō being used for the brazier (炉) and hearth (風炉).
* [[:en:Censer|Censer]] (November to April) : Contains {{ill|Nerikō|ja|練香|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nerikō|qq=}} (kneaded incense). Ceramic kōgō are used, as the moisture from nerikō can damage lacquerware, which is avoided in this case.
* {{ill|Furo (tea ceremony)|ja|風炉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furo (tea ceremony)|qq=}} (May to October) : Contains square-cut pieces of {{ill|incense wood|ja|香木|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/incense wood|qq=}}. Kōgō made from {{ill|karaki|ja|唐木|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/karaki|qq=}} or bamboo lacquerware are used.
* Year-round : Includes clamshell kōgō (e.g., [[:en:Meretrix lusoria|clam]], [[:en:Ylistrum japonicum|sunset clam]]) or metal kōgō.
== Kōgō in Buddhist Altar Fittings == In Buddhist rituals, kōgō are generally used for holding {{ill|shōkō|ja|焼香|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shōkō|qq=}} (powdered incense) or {{ill|makkō|ja|抹香|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/makkō|qq=}}. They may also be used as containers for {{ill|zukkō|ja|塗香|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zukkō|qq=}} (powdered perfume).

{{出典の明記|date=2020-04-04}} [[File:Ogata Kenzan - Incense Box - Walters 49354 - Closed.jpg|thumb|200px|[[:en:Ogata Kenzan|Ogata Kenzan]]'s incense box]] [[File:BLW Circular Box and Cover for Incense Woods.jpg|thumb|200px|Wooden incense box]] '''Kōgō''' (香合) refers to a small li11ed [[:en:Container|container]] for storing [[:en:Incense|incense]]. It is a type of [[:en:Japanese tea utensils|tea utensils]] and also a kind of {{ill|Buddhist altar fitting|ja|仏具|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buddhist altar fitting|qq=}}. It can also be written as '''Kōbuta''' (香蓋) as an alternative spelling or referred to as '''Gōsu''' or '''Gōshi''' (合子).
== Kōgō in Tea Ceremony == In the tea ceremony, a kōgō is a container used for holding incense. During a tea gathering, incense is burned in the tea room, and the kōgō serves as the container for the incense. The kōgō is an important element in tea gatherings, and during the charcoal preparation ceremony, guests may request to view the kōgō. The kōgō typically contains three pieces of incense: two are placed near the charcoal to release fragrance, while one is set aside for the viewing. Kōgō made from {{ill|karaki|ja|唐木|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/karaki|qq=}} or bamboo lacquerware are used.
* Year-round : Includes clamshell kōgō (e.g., [[:en:Meretrix lusoria|clam]], [[:en:Ylistrum japonicum|sunset clam]]) or metal kōgō.
== Kōgō in Buddhist Altar Fittings == In Buddhist rituals, kōgō are generally used for holding {{ill|shōkō|ja|焼香|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shōkō|qq=}} (powdered incense) or {{ill|makkō|ja|抹香|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/makkō|qq=}}. They may also be used as containers for {{ill|zukkō|ja|塗香|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zukkō|qq=}} (powdered perfume).

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kōgō]][[fr:Kōgō (boîte à encens)]][[ja:香合]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11667200]] Wikidata name: kōgō [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11667200]] Wikidata name: kōgō [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Referencesの明記|date=2020-04-04}} [[File:Ogata Kenzan - Incense Box - Walters 49354 - Closed.jpg|thumb|200px|[[:en:Ogata Kenzan|Ogata Kenzan]]'s incense box]] [[File:BLW Circular Box and Cover for Incense Woods.jpg|thumb|200px|Wooden incense box]] '''Kōgō''' (香合) refers to a small li11ed [[:en:Container|container]] for storing [[:en:Incense|incense]]. It is a type of [[:en:Japanese tea utensils|tea utensils]] and also a kind of {{ill|Buddhist altar fitting|ja|仏具|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buddhist altar fitting|qq=|1=Buddhist ritual object|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11377505}}. It can also be written as '''Kōbuta''' (香蓋) as an alternative spelling or referred to as '''Gōsu''' or '''Gōshi''' (合子).
== Kōgō in Tea Ceremony == In the tea ceremony, a kōgō is a container used for holding incense. During a tea gathering, incense is burned in the tea room, and the kōgō serves as the container for the incense. The kōgō is an important element in tea gatherings, and during the charcoal preparation ceremony, guests may request to view the kōgō. The kōgō typically contains three pieces of incense: two are placed near the charcoal to release fragrance, while one is set aside for the viewing. As with all tea utensils, the kōgō used depends on the season and the specific tea setting, with different kōgō being used for the brazier (炉) and hearth (風炉).
* [[:en:Censer|Censer]] (November to April) : Contains {{ill|Nerikō|ja|練香|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nerikō|qq=|1=Nerikō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11608032}} (kneaded incense). Ceramic kōgō are used, as the moisture from nerikō can damage lacquerware, which is avoided in this case.
* {{ill|Furo (tea ceremony)|ja|風炉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furo (tea ceremony)|qq=|1=Furo (tea ceremony)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665529}} (May to October) : Contains square-cut pieces of {{ill|incense wood|ja|香木|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/incense wood|qq=|1=fragrant wood|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q87216239}}. Kōgō made from {{ill|karaki|ja|唐木|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/karaki|qq=|1=precious wood (tropical hardwood)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418549}} or bamboo lacquerware are used.
* Year-round : Includes clamshell kōgō (e.g., [[:en:Meretrix lusoria|clam]], [[:en:Ylistrum japonicum|sunset clam]]) or metal kōgō.
== Kōgō in Buddhist Altar Fittings == In Buddhist rituals, kōgō are generally used for holding {{ill|shōkō|ja|焼香|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shōkō|qq=|1=burning incense|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11567689}} (powdered incense) or {{ill|makkō|ja|抹香|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/makkō|qq=|1=makkō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11497632}}. They may also be used as containers for {{ill|zukkō|ja|塗香|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zukkō|qq=|1=zukkō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428372}} (powdered perfume).

== Kōgō in Buddhist Altar Fittings == In Buddhist rituals, kōgō are generally used for holding {{ill|shōkō|ja|焼香|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shōkō|1=burning incense|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11567689}} (powdered incense) or {{ill|makkō|ja|抹香|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/makkō|1=makkō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11497632}}. They may also be used as containers for {{ill|zukkō|ja|塗香|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zukkō|1=zukkō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428372}} (powdered perfume).

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Kōgō]][[fr:Kōgō (boîte à encens)]][[ja:香合]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Kōgō]][[fr:Kōgō (boîte à encens)]][[ja:香合]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kōgō]][[fr:Kōgō (boîte à encens)]][[ja:香合]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11667200]] Wikidata name: kōgō [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11667200]] Wikidata name: kōgō [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Referencesの明記|date=2020-04-04}} [[File:Ogata Kenzan - Incense Box - Walters 49354 - Closed.jpg|thumb|200px|[[:en:Ogata Kenzan|Ogata Kenzan]]'s incense box]] [[File:BLW Circular Box and Cover for Incense Woods.jpg|thumb|200px|Wooden incense box]] '''Kōgō''' (香合) refers to a small li11ed [[:en:Container|container]] for storing [[:en:Incense|incense]]. It is a type of [[:en:Japanese tea utensils|tea utensils]] and also a kind of {{ill|Buddhist altar fitting|ja|仏具|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buddhist altar fitting|1=Buddhist ritual object|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11377505}}. It can also be written as '''Kōbuta''' (香蓋) as an alternative spelling or referred to as '''Gōsu''' or '''Gōshi''' (合子).
== Kōgō in Tea Ceremony == In the tea ceremony, a kōgō is a container used for holding incense. During a tea gathering, incense is burned in the tea room, and the kōgō serves as the container for the incense. The kōgō is an important element in tea gatherings, and during the charcoal preparation ceremony, guests may request to view the kōgō. The kōgō typically contains three pieces of incense: two are placed near the charcoal to release fragrance, while one is set aside for the viewing. As with all tea utensils, the kōgō used depends on the season and the specific tea setting, with different kōgō being used for the brazier (炉) and hearth (風炉).
* [[:en:Censer|Censer]] (November to April) : Contains {{ill|Nerikō|ja|練香|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nerikō|1=Nerikō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11608032}} (kneaded incense). Ceramic kōgō are used, as the moisture from nerikō can damage lacquerware, which is avoided in this case.
* {{ill|Furo (tea ceremony)|ja|風炉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furo (tea ceremony)|1=Furo (tea ceremony)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665529}} (May to October) : Contains square-cut pieces of {{ill|incense wood|ja|香木|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/incense wood|1=fragrant wood|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q87216239}}. Kōgō made from {{ill|karaki|ja|唐木|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/karaki|1=precious wood (tropical hardwood)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418549}} or bamboo lacquerware are used.
* Year-round : Includes clamshell kōgō (e.g., [[:en:Meretrix lusoria|clam]], [[:en:Ylistrum japonicum|sunset clam]]) or metal kōgō.
== Kōgō in Buddhist Altar Fittings == In Buddhist rituals, kōgō are generally used for holding {{ill|shōkō|ja|焼香|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shōkō|1=burning incense|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11567689}} (powdered incense) or {{ill|makkō|ja|抹香|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/makkō|1=makkō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11497632}}. They may also be used as containers for {{ill|zukkō|ja|塗香|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zukkō|1=zukkō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428372}} (powdered perfume).

<!--Koshi (lacquerware container)--><!--Koshi (lacquerware container)--> <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Kōgō}} [[fr:Kōgō (boîte à encens)]] {{wikidata link|Q11667200}} [[Category:Buddhist ritual implements]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Containers]] [[Category:Incense]] [[Category:Japanese tea utensils]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kōgō]][[fr:Kōgō (boîte à encens)]][[ja:香合]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kōgō]][[fr:Kōgō (boîte à encens)]][[ja:香合]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kōgō]][[fr:Kōgō (boîte à encens)]][[ja:香合]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11667200]] Wikidata name: kōgō [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11667200]] Wikidata name: kōgō [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Referencesの明記|date=2020-04-04}} [[File:Ogata Kenzan - Incense Box - Walters 49354 - Closed.jpg|thumb|200px|[[:en:Ogata Kenzan|Ogata Kenzan]]'s incense box]] [[File:BLW Circular Box and Cover for Incense Woods.jpg|thumb|200px|Wooden incense box]] '''Kōgō''' (香合) refers to a small li11ed [[:en:Container|container]] for storing [[:en:Incense|incense]]. It is a type of [[:en:Japanese tea utensils|tea utensils]] and also a kind of {{ill|Buddhist altar fitting|ja|仏具|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buddhist altar fitting|1=Buddhist ritual object|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11377505}}. It can also be written as '''Kōbuta''' (香蓋) as an alternative spelling or referred to as '''Gōsu''' or '''Gōshi''' (合子).
== Kōgō in Tea Ceremony == In the tea ceremony, a kōgō is a container used for holding incense. During a tea gathering, incense is burned in the tea room, and the kōgō serves as the container for the incense. The kōgō is an important element in tea gatherings, and during the charcoal preparation ceremony, guests may request to view the kōgō. The kōgō typically contains three pieces of incense: two are placed near the charcoal to release fragrance, while one is set aside for the viewing. As with all tea utensils, the kōgō used depends on the season and the specific tea setting, with different kōgō being used for the brazier (炉) and hearth (風炉).
* [[:en:Censer|Censer]] (November to April) : Contains {{ill|Nerikō|ja|練香|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nerikō|1=Nerikō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11608032}} (kneaded incense). Ceramic kōgō are used, as the moisture from nerikō can damage lacquerware, which is avoided in this case.
* {{ill|Furo (tea ceremony)|ja|風炉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furo (tea ceremony)|1=Furo (tea ceremony)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665529}} (May to October) : Contains square-cut pieces of {{ill|incense wood|ja|香木|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/incense wood|1=fragrant wood|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q87216239}}. Kōgō made from {{ill|karaki|ja|唐木|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/karaki|1=precious wood (tropical hardwood)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418549}} or bamboo lacquerware are used.
* Year-round : Includes clamshell kōgō (e.g., [[:en:Meretrix lusoria|clam]], [[:en:Ylistrum japonicum|sunset clam]]) or metal kōgō.
== Kōgō in Buddhist Altar Fittings == In Buddhist rituals, kōgō are generally used for holding {{ill|shōkō|ja|焼香|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shōkō|1=burning incense|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11567689}} (powdered incense) or {{ill|makkō|ja|抹香|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/makkō|1=makkō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11497632}}. They may also be used as containers for {{ill|zukkō|ja|塗香|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zukkō|1=zukkō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428372}} (powdered perfume).
== External Links == {{commonscat|Kōgō}} {{茶}}
{{translated page|ja|香合|version=76895538|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[fr:K%C5%8Dg%C5%8D (bo%C3%AEte %C3%A0 encens)]]
[[ja:香合]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kōgō]][[fr:Kōgō (boîte à encens)]][[ja:香合]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kōgō]][[fr:Kōgō (boîte à encens)]][[ja:香合]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kōgō]][[fr:Kōgō (boîte à encens)]][[ja:香合]]
{{Referencesの明記|date=2020-04-04}} [[File:Ogata Kenzan - Incense Box - Walters 49354 - Closed.jpg|thumb|200px|{{ill|Ogata Kenzan|en|Ogata Kenzan|WD=Q534279|lt=Ogata Kenzan}}'s incense box]] [[File:BLW Circular Box and Cover for Incense Woods.jpg|thumb|200px|Wooden incense box]] '''Kōgō''' (香合) refers to a small li11ed {{ill|Container|en|Container|WD=Q987767|lt=container}} for storing {{ill|Incense|en|Incense|WD=Q273131|lt=incense}}. It is a type of {{ill|Japanese tea utensils|en|Japanese tea utensils|WD=Q18559660|lt=tea utensils}} and also a kind of {{ill|Buddhist altar fitting|ja|仏具|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buddhist altar fitting|1=Buddhist ritual object|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11377505}}. It can also be written as '''Kōbuta''' (香蓋) as an alternative spelling or referred to as '''Gōsu''' or '''Gōshi''' (合子).
== Kōgō in Tea Ceremony == In the tea ceremony, a kōgō is a container used for holding incense. During a tea gathering, incense is burned in the tea room, and the kōgō serves as the container for the incense. The kōgō is an important element in tea gatherings, and during the charcoal preparation ceremony, guests may request to view the kōgō. The kōgō typically contains three pieces of incense: two are placed near the charcoal to release fragrance, while one is set aside for the viewing. As with all tea utensils, the kōgō used depends on the season and the specific tea setting, with different kōgō being used for the brazier (炉) and hearth (風炉).
* {{ill|Censer|en|Censer|WD=Q1264081|lt=Censer}} (November to April) : Contains {{ill|Nerikō|ja|練香|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nerikō|1=Nerikō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11608032}} (kneaded incense). Ceramic kōgō are used, as the moisture from nerikō can damage lacquerware, which is avoided in this case.
* {{ill|Furo (tea ceremony)|ja|風炉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furo (tea ceremony)|1=Furo (tea ceremony)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665529}} (May to October) : Contains square-cut pieces of {{ill|incense wood|ja|香木|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/incense wood|1=fragrant wood|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q87216239}}. Kōgō made from {{ill|karaki|ja|唐木|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/karaki|1=precious wood (tropical hardwood)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418549}} or bamboo lacquerware are used.
* Year-round : Includes clamshell kōgō (e.g., {{ill|Meretrix lusoria|en|Meretrix lusoria|WD=Q1134457|lt=clam}}, {{ill|Ylistrum japonicum|en|Ylistrum japonicum|WD=Q47148156|lt=sunset clam}}) or metal kōgō.
== Kōgō in Buddhist Altar Fittings == In Buddhist rituals, kōgō are generally used for holding {{ill|shōkō|ja|焼香|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shōkō|1=burning incense|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11567689}} (powdered incense) or {{ill|makkō|ja|抹香|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/makkō|1=makkō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11497632}}. They may also be used as containers for {{ill|zukkō|ja|塗香|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zukkō|1=zukkō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428372}} (powdered perfume).
== External Links == {{commonscat|Kōgō}} {{茶}}
{{translated page|ja|香合|version=76895538|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[fr:K%C5%8Dg%C5%8D (bo%C3%AEte %C3%A0 encens)]]
[[ja:香合]]

It is a type of {{ill|Japanese tea utensils|en|Japanese tea utensils|WD=Q18559660|lt=tea utensils|1=Japanese tea utensils}} and also a kind of {{ill|Buddhist altar fitting|ja|仏具|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buddhist altar fitting|1=Buddhist ritual object|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11377505}}. It can also be written as '''Kōbuta''' (香蓋) as an alternative spelling or referred to as '''Gōsu''' or '''Gōshi''' (合子).
== Kōgō in Tea Ceremony == In the tea ceremony, a kōgō is a container used for holding incense. During a tea gathering, incense is burned in the tea room, and the kōgō serves as the container for the incense. The kōgō is an important element in tea gatherings, and during the charcoal preparation ceremony, guests may request to view the kōgō. The kōgō typically contains three pieces of incense: two are placed near the charcoal to release fragrance, while one is set aside for the viewing. As with all tea utensils, the kōgō used depends on the season and the specific tea setting, with different kōgō being used for the brazier (炉) and hearth (風炉).
* {{ill|Censer|en|Censer|WD=Q1264081|lt=Censer|1=Censer}} (November to April) : Contains {{ill|Nerikō|ja|練香|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nerikō|1=Q11608032|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11608032}} (kneaded incense). Ceramic kōgō are used, as the moisture from nerikō can damage lacquerware, which is avoided in this case.
* {{ill|Furo (tea ceremony)|ja|風炉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furo (tea ceremony)|1=Q11665529|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665529}} (May to October) : Contains square-cut pieces of {{ill|incense wood|ja|香木|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/incense wood|1=fragrant wood|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q87216239}}. Kōgō made from {{ill|karaki|ja|唐木|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/karaki|1=precious wood (tropical hardwood)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418549}} or bamboo lacquerware are used.
* Year-round : Includes clamshell kōgō (e.g., {{ill|Meretrix lusoria|en|Meretrix lusoria|WD=Q1134457|lt=clam|1=Meretrix lusoria}}, {{ill|Ylistrum japonicum|en|Ylistrum japonicum|WD=Q47148156|lt=sunset clam|1=Ylistrum japonicum}}) or metal kōgō.
== Kōgō in Buddhist Altar Fittings == In Buddhist rituals, kōgō are generally used for holding {{ill|shōkō|ja|焼香|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shōkō|1=burning incense|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11567689}} (powdered incense) or {{ill|makkō|ja|抹香|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/makkō|1=Q11497632|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11497632}}. They may also be used as containers for {{ill|zukkō|ja|塗香|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zukkō|1=Q11428372|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428372}} (powdered perfume).
